# Source Card: AND/ASPEN Consensus Statement — Indicators for Identification and Documentation of Pediatric Malnutrition (Undernutrition)

## Summary
The 2015 AND/ASPEN consensus statement (Nutr Clin Pract 2015;30(1):147-61; PMID 25422273; DOI 10.1177/0884533614557642) recommends a standardized set of diagnostic indicators to identify and document pediatric undernutrition in routine clinical practice for children aged 1 month to 18 years, across acute, ambulatory, and residential settings. Single-data-point diagnosis uses anthropometric z-scores (weight-for-height/length, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, or MUAC); with 2+ data points it adds weight gain velocity, weight loss, deceleration in weight-for-length/height z-score, and inadequate nutrient intake. For the pediatric CDS module this source establishes the indicator vocabulary and population/setting scope; the numeric severity cutoffs (mild/moderate/severe) live in the primary's tables, which were NOT verbatim-accessible here and are logged as GAPs below rather than asserted.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Population/documentation scope = ages 1 month to 18 years — locator: Abstract, purpose sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4e0dc9439b0be3b21664ea27dc1e5189eb85903f4165c3be2755e268c24cecc4]"
- (ev_002) Single-data-point indicators = z-scores for weight-for-height/length, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, or MUAC — locator: Abstract, recommended-indicators sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:04a895d3e276555f5e69cb0573f72d18b454582e90dc37148aeffa477c1ad0cc]"
- (ev_003) 2+ data-point indicators add weight gain velocity (<2 y), weight loss (2-20 y), z-score deceleration, inadequate intake — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d66f1edb2b64aeb65a55dfe90b85fe4f5d85f2f7e98f0d9465e154d8b39a696f]"
- (ev_004) Setting scope = acute, ambulatory/outpatient, residential — locator: Abstract, settings sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d1f5b4b94f564e32c8fe274e81626c561efdbb69bfef866fd9c90c5a01e5843e]"
- (ev_005) Scope boundary — this is a diagnosis/documentation tool, not a screening tool — locator: Abstract, scope-exclusion sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:599bbf7066fe63d68cf1db3b170ffc7f10f98e94407112470609b194be8e8862]"
- (ev_006) Use as many data points as available — locator: Abstract, clinician-guidance sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:556021e326b6c4216906fb3ae002897f758f31ed74aa4c126c98c57fe803e9cc]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric, ages 1 month to 18 years (post-neonatal general pediatric); sex not stratified in scope statements; not GA/PNA-stratified.
- Assay/method: anthropometry- and intake-based indicators (weight, length/height, BMI, MUAC, serial weight/intake), NOT laboratory-assay-dependent; z-scores are computed against a growth reference (WHO vs CDC standard is NOT named in the accessed abstract — see Limitations).
- Lifecycle: 2015 AND/ASPEN consensus statement; effective 2015 (Nutr Clin Pract 30(1)); no explicit retirement or superseded-prior reference stated in the accessed abstract.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- This source explicitly scopes ITSELF OUT of screening: it defines diagnosis/documentation indicators, not nutritional-risk screening triggers (ev_005). It is therefore an input to CDS classification/documentation, not a screening/referral rule. It defines no lab red-flag; severity stratification (mild/moderate/severe) is the intended downstream classification but its numeric cutoffs are GAPs here (see Limitations).

## Limitations & conflicts
- Full text is paywalled and not in PMC; only the PubMed abstract was verbatim-accessible. The primary's numeric classification tables were NOT verbatim-locatable, so the following are recorded as GAPs (NOT asserted for PMID 25422273):
  - GAP: single-data-point severity z-score cutoffs for weight-for-length, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, and MUAC (mild/moderate/severe) — threshold not locatable in the accessed source passage.
  - GAP: weight gain velocity severity cutoffs (% of norm), weight-loss severity cutoffs (% of usual body weight), deceleration cutoffs (z-score decline of 1/2/3), and inadequate-nutrient-intake severity bands (% of estimated requirement) — thresholds not locatable in the accessed source passage.
  - Lead for follow-up (UNVERIFIED against primary; do NOT treat as source-supported fact): a secondary ASPEN educational reproduction lists single-point z-score bands as mild = -1 to -1.9 Z, moderate = -2 to -2.9 Z, severe = -3 Z or below, and 2-point cutoffs as weight loss 5% / 7.5% / 10% of usual body weight, weight gain velocity <75% / <50% / <25% of norm, deceleration decline of 1 / 2 / 3 Z, inadequate intake 51-75% / 26-50% / <25% of estimated requirement. These MUST be re-verified against the primary tables (Nutr Clin Pract 2015;30(1):147-61) before any CDS rule is proposed.
- GAP: the growth-reference standard (WHO 0-24 months vs CDC 2-20 years) used for the z-scores is not stated in the accessed abstract; do not assume.
- Preserved conflict (do NOT collapse): the abstract scopes the documentation population as "1 month to 18 years," yet the weight-loss indicator is stated as applying to "2-20 years of age" — the weight-loss age band extends past the 18-year documentation ceiling (it derives from the underlying weight-loss reference data). Record as a stated age-band inconsistency, not a single number.
